rationale: Most complex B+Tree operation implemented so far; defines the node-allocation convention and leaf/internal split-and-promote semantics that later subtasks (1.2.4 delete, 1.2.5 prefix scan, 1.2.6 persist/reload) will depend on and build atop.

modification_reason.engine/btree/lookup.go: Factored the root->leaf descent loop out of Lookup into a shared descendToLeaf helper so Insert can reuse the exact same traversal logic instead of duplicating it, per task instructions. Lookup's external behavior/signature is unchanged.

downstream_dependents: task-1.2.5 (prefix scan, relies on NextLeaf chain built by Insert's leaf splits)
task-1.2.6 (persist/reload, will need to persist the new NodeAllocator's high-water-mark and eventually the current root node ID)
known_gaps_flagged_for_future_subtasks: No mechanism yet persists 'the current root node ID' across process restarts (Insert returns newRootNodeID to the caller each call; caller is responsible for tracking it in-memory for now). This is expected to be addressed by 1.2.5/1.2.6.

NodeAllocator's sidecar high-water-mark file is durable across reopen of the same index file, but nothing yet ties it to the root-pointer persistence problem above -- flagged as a known gap for 1.2.5/1.2.6 to revisit.

Version field on nodes is round-tripped but not bumped by Insert (concurrency/optimistic-read logic is explicitly deferred to a later, concurrency-focused subtask per docs/LLD/btree.md, consistent with how 1.2.1/1.2.2 already treated it).
test_regression_scope: engine/btree/lookup_test.go (1.2.2, must still pass unchanged after descendToLeaf refactor)
